key_insight: A transformer, when given a suitable encoding, can learn to generate valid fine, regular, and star triangulations of 4D reflexive polytopes.
engineering_relevance: This suggests generative models can support automated exploration and classification of complex geometric structures relevant to Calabi-Yau manifold construction.
method: Encode triangulations so a transformer can model their combinatorial structure.
Train the model to generate new FRSTs across multiple polytope sizes.
Retrain on model outputs to improve generation quality over iterations.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports that the approach was trained and tested across a range of polytope sizes and could successfully generate representative new FRSTs.
watch_item: Generative quality may depend strongly on the encoding scheme and on whether self-generated retraining preserves validity rather than amplifying biases or errors.
